element_introduction_order.element: Basic 3-button fighting mechanics (inherited from World Heroes 1)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Carried forward from predecessor; players expected to know basics
Non-essential mechanic; strategic mind-games in later fights
Close-range positioning becomes critical against new 6 characters
Defensive counter to zoning strategies; required for certain matchups
Advanced defensive technique against throw-heavy opponents

element_introduction_order.element: Six new playable characters with unique movesets
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learning each character's patterns before facing random selection of 4 original 8
Unpredictability requires adaptive strategy; must handle varied matchups
Significant difficulty spike; advanced AI and moveset
Hardest encounter; ultimate test of all learned mechanics

element_introduction_order.element: Death Match mode with environmental hazards
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Unlockable/alternate mode after Normal Game completion
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Stage hazards add spatial complexity; seesaw lifebar changes victory conditions
Inverts knockdown recovery; positioning matters more than damage numbers

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a character beat in tournament progression
Knockdown requiring opponent button-mash recovery (Death Match)
typical_tension_triggers: Opponent projectile fired (requires reflection or evasion)
Timer running low with health tied
Seesaw lifebar arrow approaching opponent's end (Death Match)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament bracket structure creates natural pacing: escalating opponent difficulty (6 new fighters) → random selection of 4 originals (unpredictability) → semi-final and final bosses. Each round is 2-of-3, creating micro-tension cycles within macro tournament progression. Death Match mode adds environmental pressure for replay value.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Death Match mode unlocks as alternate path with environmental stage variations

difficulty_curve.early_game: Six new characters introduced sequentially; moderate challenge to learn movesets. Basic mechanics sufficient for progression.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Four randomly selected original characters (from original 8) create unpredictability. Players must adapt to unknown matchups. New mechanics (projectile reflection, counter-grab) become essential.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Neo-Geegus semi-final boss represents significant difficulty spike. Dio final boss is hardest encounter of Normal Game. Death Match mode provides extended challenge with environmental hazards and seesaw lifebar mechanic.
notable_spikes: First encounter with new 6-character movesets (moderate spike from tutorial expectations)
Random selection of 4 original 8 (unpredictability creates tension)
Death Match mode introduction (environmental hazards + seesaw mechanic)

notable_level_patterns.description: Normal Game mode follows clear progression: 6 new characters (learning phase) → 4 random original characters (adaptation phase) → Neo-Geegus (semi-final spike) → Dio (final spike). Each victory gate increases stakes and introduces new challenge types.

Each character battle requires winning 2 rounds before advancement. Timer draws create seesaw determination. Creates internal rhythm of relief (win round) and tension (next round) within single matchup.
New 6 characters force player to learn projectile reflection and counter-grabbing through necessary adaptation. Mechanics emerge from matchup requirements rather than tutorial.
Death Match mode inverts typical fighting game lifebar. Knockdown advantage becomes liability (opponent must button-mash recovery). Creates tension around knockdown timing and positioning.
